why_good: First fully original composition — no source story or character reference. Child invented her own underwater world ('I made it up, it's my own ocean') with multiple subjects (octopus, fish, crab, seaweed) in a community scene. Error recovery visible: scribbled out a mistake and continued rather than starting over. Signed name three times in different colors — identity expression integrated into art.
transferable_pattern: Original world-building with error recovery: composing a scene from imagination rather than reference, populating it with multiple interacting subjects, and persisting through mistakes. The shift from single-subject character portraits (CREATE-0001 through 0003) to a multi-subject invented scene marks a compositional step change.
tradeoffs: Less detailed coloring than previous drawings — speed and composition prioritized over polish. The error recovery (scribble-and-continue) is process capability evidence, not visible in the final product alone.
review_criteria: Observed artifact (evidence tier 4). Original drawing with child commentary confirming it was self-invented. Flexibility score 4/5 (first original world-building).
